name: Common
note: Spoken by Humans, Halflings, Half-Elves, and Half-Orcs. The trade language of the realms, Common uses the **Common Script** and is known by nearly every civilized creature.

name: Dwarvish
note: Spoken by Dwarves. Full of hard consonants and gutteral sounds, Dwarvish is written in the **Dwarvish Script** (Dethek runes). It is often used for technical and engineering documents.

name: Elvish
note: Spoken by Elves. Fluid, melodic, and subtle, Elvish is written in the **Elvish Script** (Espruar). It is the language of high culture, poetry, and song.

name: Giant
note: Spoken by Giants, Ogres, and Trolls. It uses a variation of the **Dwarvish Script**. It is an ancient, booming language, often simplified by lesser giant-kin.

name: Gnomish
note: Spoken by Gnomes. It uses the **Dwarvish Script** and is renowned for its technical vocabulary and capacity for extremely specific nuances.

name: Goblin
note: Spoken by Goblins, Hobgoblins, and Bugbears. Using the **Dwarvish Script**, it is a rough, jagged language often spoken quickly and hushed.

name: Halfling
note: Spoken by Halflings. It uses the **Common Script**. It is not a secret language, but Halflings loathe sharing it with others. It has little written tradition.

name: Orc
note: Spoken by Orcs. Written in the **Dwarvish Script**, it is a harsh, discordant language with no words for 'peace' or 'mercy'.

name: Abyssal
note: Spoken by Demons. Written in the **Infernal Script**, it differs by being a chaotic, screaming tongue that can cause madness in mortals.

name: Celestial
note: Spoken by Celestials (Angels). Written in the **Celestial Script**, it sounds like a choir singing, even when spoken normally.

name: Draconic
note: Spoken by Dragons and Dragonborn. Written in the **Draconic Script** (Iokharic). It is one of the oldest languages, often used in the study of magic.

name: Deep Speech
note: Spoken by Mind Flayers and Beholders. It has **No Script** and represents the alien mind of the Far Realm. The sound of it is unsettling to terrestrial ears.

name: Infernal
note: Spoken by Devils. Written in the **Infernal Script**. Precise, legalistic, and hissing, it is a language of contracts and deception.

name: Primordial
note: Spoken by Elementals. Written in the **Dwarvish Script**. It includes the dialects of Aquan, Auran, Ignan, and Terran.

name: Sylvan
note: Spoken by Fey creatures. Written in the **Elvish Script**. It is whimsical and old, seemingly part of the natural world itself.

name: Undercommon
note: Spoken by Drow and Underdark traders. Written in the **Elvish Script**. A mix of Elvish, Dwarvish, and Surface Common, adapted for life in the dark.

name: Druidic
note: The secret language of Druids. Only Druids know it, and teaching it to an outsider is forbidden. It has its own unique script.

name: Thieves' Cant
note: A secret mix of dialect, jargon, and code used by Rogues to hide messages in seemingly normal conversation.
